item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because I've got an odd-but-legit phobia of plesiosaurs, but this good doggo had me charmed! …and hating MC&D even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is it Safe? If there is many feral species of SCP-3934, what even can be hostile, it must be Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agent Rourke: Lad, did you just play fetch with the damn Loch Ness Monster?

This line sums up my reaction to this. This is actually amazing but also so crazy (to me at least.) It's MC&D too and I always like to see what crazy, anomalous stuff they come up with to sell and make a profit from. Take an upvote as I regret that I can't have a cute Loch Ness Puppy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was cute but also very sad. Reminds of Sea World and Orcas doing dog tricks. Upvote!
Woohoo! Thanks everbody :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://hisclockworkservants.tumblr.com/image/168237803204

Sunny made this awesome art of this skip!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: F**king adorable. Also, why is the discovery in a collapsible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aquatic Dino puppers must be protected at all costs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course MC&D ran a puppy mill for plesiosaurs, the bastards.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn good story!

I always was sotf on abandoned pets, and this story got to me strong.
Heartless rich bustards! for throwing them out like that.
And the Foundation playing anomalius WWF, shows that this guys have a heart after all.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta love a good ol' aquatic golden retriever ;-;

+1 with a fish

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More cryptids on the site is definitely a good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this idea. I've always liked cryptids and dinosaur-era animals. The story was good, and the execution was wonderful.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
produced via anomalous means by Marshall, Carter, and Dark LLP

They auction off most of what they create, right?
How much do you think this would go for?

I'm thinking at least $3.50

On a serious note, the scientific tone of this was really good, along with being really interesting. The image is really good quality too, and works on my heavily restricted network.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I probably had a bit too much fun with the log, but if you aren't enjoying yourself what's the point of writing eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mini-Loch Ness Monsters seem like the kind of thing that should have been on the wiki ages ago. Good job making this a reality, focusing the article on how they're made and tying into all kinds of history. Though I'll say the log at the end is kind of silly. (By jove, they're all terribly British, what, what!) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, I was upset at the first half because of the poor treatment, and grinned throughout the log. Can't believe the Loch Ness monster can be so cute. Hearty +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's possible I said something like that, though I only decided to work on this fairly recently. Not sure honestly.

Either way I'm glad you enjoyed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember someone making a comment about making a Loch Ness monster SCP over on the subreddit a couple months back. Was that you?

Either way fantastic job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed the log :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slowly, SCP-3934-1 moves closer, before beginning to eat the fish out of O'Doherty's hand. Specimen seems hesitant at first, but quickly gains enthusiasm. After consuming the fish, it moves forward and begins to nuzzle O'Doherty's leg with its neck.

nooooooooo

As Rourke leaves the room, SCP-3934-1 briefly submerges before returning with a ball held in its mouth. It then moves towards the pool's edge and deposits the ball in front of O'Doherty. O'Doherty then throws the ball towards the other end of the pool. Rourke returns just as SCP-3934-1 promptly retrieves the ball and swims back to O'Doherty.

NOOOOOOOOOOOOO

I can't. ;^;

+1 for the sad fate exotic nessie

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to Weryllium, Hippo, Not a Seagull, Drchandra, and Zyn for critique. This skip is intended as an allegory for the real life issues faced by exotic pets such as turtles or wolf-like dogs. These animals are difficult to care and provide for, and often end up dead or abandoned.

+ Image Attribution
- Hide

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Loch_Ness_Monster_02.jpg
Picture is an edited version of this public domain image.

If you enjoyed, check out my other stuff here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/modern-erasmus-author-page
